list.text: Elite Technical Architect
Performance & Architecture Lead

box.title: Aravind Govindhasamy

list.text: Slow: High JS execution on startup.
Expensive: Downloading code that might never run.

box.title: Traditional frameworks 'hydrate' on the client, essentially re-running the app to make it interactive.

list.text: Zero Hydration: No upfront execution cost.
Instant TTI: App is interactive as soon as the HTML arrives.

box.title: Qwik 'pauses' execution on the server and 'resumes' it on the client without downloading or executing JS upfront.

list.text: Scalable: Perfect for massive e-commerce sites.
Predictable: Performance remains elite as the app grows.

box.title: Qwik's performance is constant relative to the size of the application. More components don't mean slower starts.

box.code:
// 컴포넌트가 아닌 지연 로딩 가능한 참조
const onButtonClick = $(() => console.log('Clicked!'));
box.title: Qwik Resolution Locator. A string that identifies how to lazy-load and execute code on demand.
Defined with a trailing dollar sign, these components are automatically split by the optimizer for lazy loading.

list.text: useSignal: Simple value reactivity.
useStore: Deeply reactive objects.

box.title: Reactive primitives that manage state and trigger updates precisely where they are needed.

list.text: Stateful HTML: The UI knows exactly where it left off.
Pure Resumability: No JS execution required until an event occurs.

box.title: Qwik serializes the application state and the execution listeners into the HTML.

list.text: Proven: Years of production testing.
Middleware: Access to thousands of existing plugins.

box.title: The most mature and widely used Node.js framework, providing a stable foundation for complex APIs.
Integrating Express middleware directly into Qwik City's server-side processing.

list.text: Unified: One backend, multiple consumption points.
Reliable: Standard Express routing patterns.

box.title: Building robust RESTful APIs that serve both your Qwik frontend and external clients.
Centralized error management using Express middleware to ensure consistent API responses.

slides.title: 4. Seamless Data Handling
slides.subtitle: Route Loaders and Actions in Qwik City.

box.title: Functions that run on the server to fetch data before a route is rendered, providing full type safety.
Handles form submissions and data mutations with built-in validation and state management.

list.text: Shared Models: Use the same Pydantic/Zod schemas everywhere.
Validated: Catch type errors at compile time.

box.title: Loaders and Actions automatically infer types, ensuring your frontend is always in sync with your backend data.

list.text: Gateway: Express handles auth and rate limiting.
Streaming: Proxying data efficiently to the client.

box.title: Using Express to tunnel requests to microservices while maintaining Qwik's resumability benefits.

slides.title: 5. The Qwik Optimizer
slides.subtitle: Under the Hood: How Qwik stays small and fast.

list.text: Dynamic Splitting: Code is only downloaded when needed.
Tree Shaking: Removing unreachable server code from the client bundle.

box.title: A tool that analyzes your code and splits it into tiny, independent 'QRL' chunks.

list.text: Predictive: Smart algorithms identify user intent.
Background: Downloads happen during idle time, avoiding lag.

box.title: Qwik speculatively fetches the code for components that are likely to be interacted with.

box.code:
// Code stays in the cache until the event fires
const onClick = $(() => { ... });
box.title: Code is not only lazy-loaded but also lazy-executed, minimizing the CPU work on the client.

list.text: Minimal CSS: Only ship the styles for visible components.
Reusable: Build a component-driven design system with ease.

box.title: Object-Oriented CSS patterns in Qwik ensure that styles are as resumable and optimized as the logic.

slides.title: 6. Deep Reactivity
slides.subtitle: Signals, Stores, and Context in Qwik.

box.code:
const count = useSignal(0);
const state = useStore({ users: [] });
box.title: Use `useSignal` for primitive values and `useStore` for complex, deeply nested objects.

box.code:
const AuthContext = createContextId('auth');
const auth = useContext(AuthContext);
box.title: Share reactive state across your entire component tree without prop drilling.

list.text: useTask$: Runs on both server and client.
useVisibleTask$: Runs only on the client when visible.

box.title: Use `useTask$` and `useVisibleTask$` to handle side effects and data synchronization.
Passing signals and stores as props allows child components to react to state changes efficiently.

slides.title: 7. Designing the Future
slides.subtitle: Tailwind, CSS Modules, and Resumable CSS.

list.text: Fast: Rapidly build UIs without leaving your HTML.
Consistent: Leverage a robust design system.

box.title: The utility-first framework that fits perfectly into Qwik's component-based architecture.
Using `useStylesScpoted$` to ensure styling is encapsulated within your component.

list.text: Static: CSS is delivered in the initial HTML.
Clean: No runtime style generation costs.

box.title: Qwik prefers static CSS or compiled CSS-in-JS to maintain its zero-JS-at-startup guarantee.
Using the `style` prop for dynamic styling while ensuring the optimizer can still do its work.

slides.title: 8. Global Delivery
slides.subtitle: Adapters, Qwik City, and the Edge.

box.code:
// npx qwik add cloudflare-pages
// npx qwik add netlify-edge
box.title: Deploy your Qwik application anywhere with a single command using built-in adapters.

list.text: Localized: Serve users from the nearest data center.
Secure: Perform authentication at the edge.

box.title: Leveraging the Edge to handle localized data fetching and security headers before the user's request reaches the origin.

list.text: Dynamic: SSR for personalized content.
Fast: SSG for static landing pages.

box.title: Combining SSR, SSG, and CSR to achieve the perfect balance of performance and flexibility.
Using Qwik's strictly-typed environment variables to ensure production configuration is reliable.
Fast, native unit tests for your Qwik components and signal logic.

list.text: Reliable: Test complex user journeys across routes.
Visual: Capture screenshots and videos of failures.

box.title: Automating browser interactions to ensure your full-stack flows work perfectly in the real world.
Using tools like MSW (Mock Service Worker) to simulate your Express backend during frontend development and testing.

list.text: Scalable: Ensure your app grows gracefully.
Insights: Monitor CPU and memory usage during stress tests.

box.title: Verifying that your Express server can handle high traffic volumes and identifying performance bottlenecks.

box.code:
// shared/schemas.ts
export const UserSchema = z.object({ id: z.string() });
box.title: Using Zod to define schemas that can be used on the server, the client, and within loaders for 100% consistency.

list.text: Traceable: Debug issues faster with clear logs.
Analyzable: Export logs to systems like ELK or Datadog.

box.title: Integrating Morgan or Winston into your Express server for detailed request and error logging.
A centralized approach to managing secrets and configurations across the full stack.

list.text: Modular: Types evolve with your business logic.
Strong: Prevent accidental data leakages.

box.title: Using TypeScript utility types (`Pick`, `Omit`, `Partial`) to strictly define data shapes for different layers of your app.

list.text: Resumability: The next step in web performance.
Express: The solid foundation for every project.
Tooling: Exceptional DX with Qwik City and Node.js.

box.title: Qwik and Express provide a unique combination of cutting-edge resumability and industry-standard backend reliability.

list.text: Growth: Qwik is quickly gaining a loyal following.
Optimization: Expect even smaller bundles and faster delivery.

box.title: Keep an eye on the continued evolution of resumability and the move towards more specialized edge computing runtimes.
Resumability is not just a feature; it's a paradigm shift. Start building with Qwik and Express today!
